title: What "Hard on Yourself" Actually Means in Scripture
caption:
Somewhere along the way, "be hard on yourself" turned into a license for self-hatred. Scripture never taught that.

Self-hatred says "I'm garbage." Conviction says "that was wrong." One buries you. The other moves you. David didn't wallow after Nathan confronted him. "I acknowledged my sin to you." (Psalm 32:5) He confessed and got up. Godly sorrow does its work and moves.

And the standard runs one direction: patience for your brother's failure, honesty for your own. Most guys run that backwards.

"There is now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus." (Romans 8:1) Being hard on yourself was never the destination. It's the road that gets you to grace you actually receive.

Save this for the next time conviction starts sounding like contempt.

#faith #christianmen #repentance #godlymen #manofgod #scripture #grace
theme: He's hard on himself, gentle with others.
